No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to hunger, food security, or agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to health, well-being, or related targets.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops technical skills in digital technology and problem-solving (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to gender equality or related targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to water, sanitation, or related targets.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course fosters innovation and digital technology understanding, contributing to technological upgrading and higher economic productivity (Target 8.2) and decent job creation.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is fundamental to information technology development, fostering innovation and upgrading technological capabilities in industrial sectors (Target 9.5, 9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to reducing inequalities or promoting inclusion.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: While Dijkstra's algorithm can apply to transport, the syllabus does not explicitly link it to sustainable transport systems or urban planning, making the inference too weak.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Computational efficiency, while beneficial, does not directly translate to sustainable management of natural resources (Target 12.2) without explicit context in the syllabus.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to climate change, adaptation, or mitigation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course promotes 'professional and ethical responsibility' and 'Respect Copyright,' which are minor contributions to the rule of law and strong institutions (Target 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course aims to cultivate 'teamwork ability' and an 'international perspective,' foundational skills for global partnerships and cooperation (Target 17.6, 17.16).
